# terrible_amazon_aws_ec2_vpc_nacl (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `egress` (String) A list of rules for outgoing traffic. Each rule must be specified as a list. Each rule may contain the rule number (integer 1-32766), protocol (one of ['tcp', 'udp', 'icmp', 'ipv6-icmp', '-1', 'all']), the rule action ('allow' or 'deny') the CIDR of the IPv4 or IPv6 network range to allow or deny, the ICMP type (-1 means all types), the ICMP code (-1 means all codes), the last port in the range for TCP or UDP protocols, and the first port in the range for TCP or UDP protocols. See examples.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `ingress` (String) List of rules for incoming traffic. Each rule must be specified as a list. Each rule may contain the rule number (integer 1-32766), protocol (one of ['tcp', 'udp', 'icmp', 'ipv6-icmp', '-1', 'all']), the rule action ('allow' or 'deny') the CIDR of the IPv4 or IPv6 network range to allow or deny, the ICMP type (-1 means all types), the ICMP code (-1 means all codes), the last port in the range for TCP or UDP protocols, and the first port in the range for TCP or UDP protocols. See examples.
- `nacl_id` (String) NACL id identifying a network ACL. One and only one of the `name` or `nacl_id` is required. Mutually exclusive with `name`.
- `name` (String) Tagged name identifying a network ACL. One and only one of the `name` or `nacl_id` is required. Mutually exclusive with `nacl_id`.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) Creates or modifies an existing NACL. Deletes a NACL and reassociates subnets to the default NACL.
- `subnets` (String) The list of subnets that should be associated with the network ACL. Must be specified as a list Each subnet can be specified as subnet ID, or its tagged name.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `vpc_id` (String) VPC id of the requesting VPC. Required when state present.

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
